def solve(nums, k)
    N = nums.length
    INF = Float::INFINITY

    # Maximum possible peaks is floor(N/2)
    if k > N / 2.0
        return -1
    end
    if k == 0
        return 0
    end

    # Step 1: Calculate costs C_i
    # C_i is the minimum cost to make nums[i] a peak, assuming neighbors are not modified.
    C = Array.new(N)
    for i in 0...N
        # Circular indices
        prev = nums[(i - 1 + N) % N]
        next_val = nums[(i + 1) % N]
        
        required_height = [prev, next_val].max + 1
        C[i] = [0, required_height - nums[i]].max
    end

    # Helper function to solve Minimum Weight Independent Set (MWIS) of size targetK on a path graph
    def solve_path(W, targetK)
        L = W.length
        
        if targetK < 0
            return INF
        elsif targetK == 0
            return 0
        elsif L == 0
            return INF
        elsif L < targetK
            return INF
        end

        # DP[i][j][s]: Min cost using W[0..i], selecting j nodes.
        # s=0: W[i] not selected.
        # s=1: W[i] selected.
        
        # dp_prev[j] = [cost_j_not_selected, cost_j_selected]
        dp_prev = Array.new(targetK + 1) { [INF, INF] }
        dp_curr = Array.new(targetK + 1) { [INF, INF] }

        # Base case i=0
        dp_prev[0][0] = 0 # W[0] not selected, 0 items, cost 0
        if 1 <= targetK
            dp_prev[1][1] = W[0] # W[0] selected, 1 item, cost W[0]
        end

        for i in 1...L
            # Reset current DP row
            dp_curr.each { |row| row[0] = INF; row[1] = INF }

            for j in 0..targetK
                # Case s=0: W[i] not selected
                # Transition from (i-1) selected or not selected
                dp_curr[j][0] = [dp_prev[j][0], dp_prev[j][1]].min

                # Case s=1: W[i] selected
                if j >= 1
                    # Must transition from (i-1) not selected
                    cost = dp_prev[j - 1][0] + W[i]
                    dp_curr[j][1] = cost
                end
            end
            # Move current to previous for next iteration
            dp_prev, dp_curr = dp_curr, dp_prev
        end

        # The result is stored in dp_prev after L-1 iterations
        result = [dp_prev[targetK][0], dp_prev[targetK][1]].min
        return result
    end

    # --- Cycle Handling ---

    min_total_cost = INF

    # Case 1: Index 0 is NOT selected (S_0_out)
    # Solve MWIS on Path [1, ..., N-1] for exactly k peaks.
    if N >= 2
        weights_out = C[1...N]
        cost1 = solve_path(weights_out, k)
        min_total_cost = [min_total_cost, cost1].min
    end


    # Case 2: Index 0 IS selected (S_0_in)
    # Requires k-1 more peaks from Path [2, ..., N-2].
    if k >= 1
        cost2_path = INF
        
        if N <= 3
             # If N=2, k=1: Path is empty, need 0 more peaks.
             # If N=3, k=1: Path is empty, need 0 more peaks.
             if N == 2 && k == 1
                 cost2_path = 0
             elsif N == 3 && k == 1
                 cost2_path = 0
             else
                 # If N=2, k>1 (impossible by check above). If N=3, k>1 (impossible by check above).
                 # If k=1 and N=2/3, we covered it. Otherwise, the required K-1 is impossible on an empty path.
                 cost2_path = INF
             end
        else # N >= 4
            # Path is [2, ..., N-2]. Length N-3.
            weights_in = C[2...(N - 1)]
            path_cost = solve_path(weights_in, k - 1)
            cost2_path = path_cost
        end
        
        if cost2_path != INF
            total_cost2 = C[0] + cost2_path
            min_total_cost = [min_total_cost, total_cost2].min
        end
    end


    return min_total_cost == INF ? -1 : min_total_cost
end

        C[i] = [0, required_height - nums[i]].max
    end

    def solve_path(W, targetK)

            return INF
        elsif targetK == 0
            return 0
        elsif L == 0
            return INF

        # s=0: W[i] not selected.
        # s=1: W[i] selected.
        
        # dp_prev[j] = [cost_j_not_selected, cost_j_selected]
        dp_prev = Array.new(targetK + 1) { [INF, INF] }
        dp_curr = Array.new(targetK + 1) { [INF, INF] }

        # Base case i=0
        dp_prev[0][0] = 0 # W[0] not selected, 0 items, cost 0
        if 1 <= targetK
            dp_prev[1][1] = W[0] # W[0] selected, 1 item, cost W[0]
        end

        for i in 1...L
            # Reset current DP row
            dp_curr.each { |row| row[0] = INF; row[1] = INF }

            for j in 0..targetK

                # Transition from (i-1) selected or not selected
                dp_curr[j][0] = [dp_prev[j][0], dp_prev[j][1]].min

                # Case s=1: W[i] selected
                if j >= 1
                    # Must transition from (i-1) not selected
                    cost = dp_prev[j - 1][0] + W[i]
                    dp_curr[j][1] = cost
                end
            end

            dp_prev, dp_curr = dp_curr, dp_prev
        end

        end
    end

        return -1
    else
        return min_total_cost
    end

Result: $\min(DP[L-1][K][0], DP[L-1][K][1])$.

---
**Main logic for Cycle $C_N$**

Case 1: Index 0 is NOT selected ($S_{0\_out}$).
We solve MWIS on the path $1, 2, \dots, N-1$.
Costs used: $C_1, C_2, \dots, C_{N-1}$. Length $L = N-1$. Target $K=k$.
Cost $R_1 = \text{solve\_path}(C[1..N-1], N-1, k)$.

Case 2: Index 0 IS selected ($S_{0\_in}$).
We solve MWIS on the path $2, 3, \dots, N-2$.
Costs used: $C_2, C_3, \dots, C_{N-2}$. Length $L = N-3$. Target $K=k-1$ (since $C_0$ is already counted).
Cost $R_{2\_path} = \text{solve\_path}(C[2..N-2], N-3, k-1)$.
$R_2 = C_0 + R_{2\_path}$.

Handle edge cases:
If $N=2$: $C_0, C_1$. We need $k=1$ peak. Cost = $\min(C_0, C_1)$.
If $N=3$: $k=1$. $C_0, C_1, C_2$.
$S_{0\_out}$: Path $1, 2$. $L=2$. Target $K=1$. $\min(C_1, C_2)$.
$S_{0\_in}$: Path empty ($2..1$). $L=0$. Target $K=0$. $R_{2\_path}=0$. Cost $C_0$.
Result: $\min(C_0, C_1, C_2)$.

If $N=4$: $k=2$. $C_0, C_1, C_2, C_3$.
$S_{0\_out}$: Path $1, 2, 3$. $L=3$. Target $K=2$.
$S_{0\_in}$: Path $2$. $L=1$. Target $K=1$. Cost $C_0 + C_2$.
Result: $\min(R_1, R_2)$.

This DP approach seems robust for handling the minimum cost $k$-independent set on a cycle.
